Vuyargur has received another golem repair request.

※Please note that the difficulty of this quest has been synced to your current level. Furthermore, you may not proceed with a class or job that is different from when you accepted this quest.

1: Speak with Xarhorkur at Zirgorteh the Open-armed.

2: Deliver the requested items to the golem tinkerer.

24: Speak with Xarhorkur at Zirgorteh the Open-armed.

48: Forgive me, <Split(<Highlight>ObjectParameter(1)</Highlight>, ,1)/>, but could I entrust you with more golem maintenance? Though its function is restored, a construct of its age needs regular upkeep.

49: In the time since your last repair, a new malfunction has arisen, and it seems more parts will need replacing.

50: Xarhorkur should have more information for you. You'll find her at Zirgorteh the Open-armed.

51: Glad you're here, <Split(<Highlight>ObjectParameter(1)</Highlight>, ,1)/>. The components we need are rather intricate, which is why I requested you and your deft hands for the job.

52: Lest you worry, I've got all the materials you need to forge them right here. Let me know if you run out!

53: Lest you worry, I've got all the materials you need to construct them right here. Let me know if you run out!

54: Lest you worry, I've got all the materials you need to prepare them right here. Let me know if you run out!

55: Oh, and once you've finished, go ahead and take them to the tinkerer for installation.

56: Need more materials? ...No, evidently not. Carry on, then! The tinkerer will be glad to see you!

59: Need more materials? Certainly! Here you are.

62: Hrmmm... Perhaps I ought to fine-tune the motor component...?

63: Your presence is a welcome one, <If(PlayerParameter(4))>miss<Else/>mister</If> artisan. Have you a job in need of the golem's strength?

64: Oh, you're a saint! With any luck, these replacement parts will stop it from making that dreadful grinding sound... The keeper will be thrilled!

65: Oh, your timing couldn't be better! Whatever was causing that delayed response time, these replacement parts ought to fix it. The keeper will be thrilled!

66: Granted, we're still learning through trial and error at this stage, so we may have need to call upon you again, if that's all right. Many thanks!

67: Once again, our golem is restored by your hand. Thank you, <Split(<Highlight>ObjectParameter(1)</Highlight>, ,1)/>.

68: Doubtless you have witnessed similar constructs in the region of Yak T'el─the former guardians of the old quarries.

69: I should like to see them for myself one day, to take comfort in the unweathered vestiges of our ancestors.

70: Much like our sepulchres, the structures we build are bearers of memory. It is a golem's duty to ensure they last for generations, and ours to ensure the golems do the same.
